opinion.h1: FORD MOTOR CO. v. PACE, 364 U.S. 444 (1960)
opinion.h4: FORD MOTOR CO. v. PACE ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF TENNESSEE. No. 431. Decided November 21, 1960.
opinion.p: Appeal dismissed for want of a properly presented federal question.
William T. Gossett, L. Homer Surbeck and Cecil Sims for appellant.
K. Harland Dodson, Jr. for appellees.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a properly presented federal question.
opinion.h4: DART DRUG CORP. OF MARYLAND v. GADOL, 364 U.S. 444 (1960)
opinion.p: 364 U.S. 444 DART DRUG CORP. OF MARYLAND ET AL. v. GADOL ET AL., DOING BUSINESS AS FOUR CORNERS PHARMACY. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF APPEALS OF MARYLAND. No. 435. Decided November 21, 1960.
Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
Joseph S. Kaufman and Stedman Prescott, Jr. for appellees.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
